title: GET /api/perf/recent returns recent perf stats
description: Dev-only endpoint exposing the perf ring buffer. Returns last 100 spans + computed p50/p95/p99 per named span. Gated by AUTOCUE_PERF env var.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/perf/recent returns 404 when AUTOCUE_PERF is unset.
When enabled: returns 200 with {spans: [{name, duration_ms, start_ts}, ...], stats: {name: {count, p50, p95, p99}, ...}}.
Endpoint is read-only and has constant-time response (no DB, just the in-memory buffer).
Listed in routes.py but not in the OpenAPI spec when disabled.

steps.details: class PerfSpan(BaseModel): name: str; duration_ms: float; start_ts: float. class PerfStat(BaseModel): count: int; p50: float; p95: float; p99: float. class PerfResponse(BaseModel): spans: list[PerfSpan]; stats: dict[str, PerfStat].
@app.get('/api/perf/recent', response_model=PerfResponse): if not perf._enabled: raise HTTPException(404). Collect last 100 from perf._buffer; group by name for stats.
tests/test_serve_routes.py: with AUTOCUE_PERF unset, 404. With AUTOCUE_PERF=1, 200 + valid schema.

technicalNotes: Don't include this in the default OpenAPI schema when disabled - users shouldn't see a phantom dev endpoint. Use include_in_schema=False conditional.
